name: Electric Motor
tagline: Magnetic fields and current-carrying conductors producing continuous rotary torque without on-site combustion
problem:
Every factory wanted shafts that spun without line belts snaking through ceilings. Steam engines at each machine were loud, hot, and fuel-hungry. The electric motor solves local motion by importing power through wires — decoupling the fire from the tool.

Design challenges span magnetic circuit design, conductor insulation in slots, bearing currents from PWM drives, and thermal paths in enclosed frames. Starting inrush can collapse weak feeders; variable speed historically required expensive secondary resistors until electronics arrived.

Without motors, there is no pump hall, no elevator, no subway — only mechanical shafting or animals.
overview:
DC motors commutate coil segments so torque stays aligned with field; universal motors run on AC for small appliances at cost of brush wear. Induction motors — the workhorse of industry — use a squirrel-cage rotor whose bars see slip-frequency currents induced by the stator’s rotating field. Synchronous machines lock to grid frequency via damper windings during pull-in.

Windings are inserted in laminated steel slots to guide flux and reduce eddy losses; insulation classes (B, F, H) set temperature ceilings. Cooling may be TEFC fins, open drip-proof, or forced air through internal ducts. Large motors use sleeve bearings with oil rings; small ones use ball bearings.

Modern drives add VFDs that synthesise variable frequency — returning us to variable speed without resistive waste, but introducing harmonics and bearing EDM from capacitive coupling.

Nameplate data — efficiency, power factor, NEMA design letters — encode starting behaviour as surely as stall torque on a steam piston.

principles.name: Lorentz force
principles.explanation: Current crossing magnetic field experiences force — the microscopic origin of motor torque.

principles.name: Slip in induction machines
principles.explanation: Rotor must lag synchronous speed to induce rotor current; at synchronous speed torque collapses to zero.

principles.name: Back-EMF
principles.explanation: Spinning conductors generate voltage opposing applied voltage — limits no-load current and sets constant-speed behaviour on stiff grids.

principles.name: Thermal class
principles.explanation: Insulation life halves roughly every 10°C over rated — derating altitude and ambient is mandatory.

components.name: Stator core and windings
components.madeFrom: Silicon steel laminations, magnet wire, varnish

components.name: Rotor and shaft
components.function: Converts magnetic torque to mechanical output
components.madeFrom: Aluminium or copper cage, steel shaft

components.name: Bearings and end bells
components.function: Supports rotor; seals windings from environment
components.madeFrom: Ball or sleeve bearings; cast aluminium bells

components.name: Terminal box
components.function: Connects supply to windings; may include star-delta links

components.name: Fan and cooling path
components.function: Removes copper and iron losses as heat

rawMaterials.name: Magnet wire
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Aluminium magnet wire in large machines
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Random wound or form wound coils

rawMaterials.name: Aluminium for rotors
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Die casting under vacuum to reduce porosity

buildSteps.title: Stack and weld core
buildSteps.description: Interleave laminations; weld or cleat back iron; insulate slots with liners.

buildSteps.title: Wind or insert coils
buildSteps.description: Maintain wire tension; tie cords; varnish dip or VPI for void fill.

buildSteps.title: Assemble rotor and balance
buildSteps.description: Cast or fabricate cage; machine OD; balance to ISO grade for speed.

buildSteps.title: Fit bearings and align load
buildSteps.description: Soft foot checks on base; coupling alignment to driven machine.
buildSteps.warningNote: Thermal growth shifts alignment — hot check

buildSteps.title: Test run and measure
buildSteps.description: Megger windings; no-load current; vibration spectrum; bearing temperature rise.
buildSteps.warningNote: VFD-fed motors need cable reflection checks

history.event: First practical rotating electric motors demonstrated in laboratory contexts.
Tesla polyphase induction motor patents underpin modern cage motors.
Three-phase transmission in Frankfurt links motors to remote generation.

inventors.name: Nikola Tesla
inventors.contribution: Polyphase induction and AC motor concepts

impact:
Motors made electricity actionable at every machine scale — from dental drills to ship propulsion. They are the distributed muscle of electrification.

Recovery must preserve lamination stamping and varnish impregnation, not only copper.

videos.title: Induction motor principles
videos.description: Accredited electrical engineering animations of rotating fields and slip.
